content:
{
  "title": "Cross-Lingual Code-Switch Prompting for Enhanced Multilingual Language Model Performance",
  "problem": "Large language models often underperform on tasks involving low-resource languages due to insufficient training data and limited linguistic overlap with high-resource languages. Existing prompting methods rarely exploit cross-lingual knowledge transfer at the prompt level.",
  "motivation": "Improving LLM capabilities for low-resource and multilingual settings is crucial for equitable NLP progress. Recently, code-switching—mixing two languages within a prompt—has shown promise in human communication and small-scale NLP models for bridging linguistic gaps. Leveraging this phenomenon as a structured prompting strategy could unlock latent multilingual capabilities in pre-trained LLMs.",
  "method": "We propose 'Cross-Lingual Code-Switch Prompts' (CLCP), where prompts explicitly interleave high-resource and target low-resource language tokens at syntactic phrase boundaries. The method includes an automatic alignment module that generates code-switched prompts from parallel data, balancing semantic fidelity and linguistic mixing. We investigate both zero-shot and few-shot scenarios, evaluating whether CLCP enhances knowledge transfer and contextual understanding for the low-resource language. Ablation experiments will study the effect of different code-switching granularities and positions in the prompt.",
  "experiment_plan": "We will evaluate CLCP on multilingual benchmarks such as XTREME and FLORES-101, focusing on tasks like translation, QA, and NER for low-resource languages. Baselines include standard monolingual prompts, vanilla translation prompts, and mixed-language paraphrasing. Metrics include accuracy, F1, BLEU, and cross-lingual transfer scores to quantify improvements in low-resource language performance."
}
